title: Install the Z-Axis Gearbox and Set Belt Tension
summary: Reinstall the Z-axis gearbox, reconnect the timing pulley, belt, motor, and idler assembly, set belt tension to the documented specification, reattach belt clamps, reinstall panels, and restart the operator station using the referenced source procedures.
when_to_use: Use when restoring the machine after Z-axis gearbox replacement or removal and the source procedure section 7.3.7.2 Z-Axis Gearbox Installation applies.
not_for: Do not use if the gearbox cannot be fully seated in the pilot hole.
Do not use if the clamp collar access hole cannot be oriented up as documented.
Do not use if the motor electrical connectors cannot be oriented down as documented.
Do not use if equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained.
Do not use if belt tension cannot be brought into the documented 448 N to 494 N range.
Do not use if three acceptable belt tension readings in a row cannot be obtained.
Do not use if the belt clamp cannot align with the belt teeth or if the belt is being pinched.

responsibilities: Perform mechanical installation of the Z-axis gearbox and related drive components.
Set and verify belt tension using the documented method and values.
Reinstall panels and restart the operator station using the referenced procedure.

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked support-safe in the candidate.
Be aware that excessive area noise can interfere with sonic meter readings.
The source section is installation-only and assumes prior removal steps from the larger gearbox replacement procedure.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Z-axis gearbox assembly
Six M6 screws for the A-axis belt clamp
Six M6 screws for the counterbalance belt clamp
Torque tool capable of 7 Nm and 21.2 Nm
Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter if used
Access to the referenced Installation procedure on page 168
Access to the referenced Motor Installation procedure on page 108
Access to the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure on page 66

access_required: Access to the Z-axis gearbox assembly
Access to the referenced Installation procedure on page 168
Access to the referenced Motor Installation procedure on page 108
Access to the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure on page 66
system_preconditions: The machine is in a state where Z-axis gearbox installation can be performed.
Prior removal steps have already been completed.
external_references: Starting The Operator Station procedure on page 66

visual_references.description: Gearbox maintenance figure that can support gearbox identification and installation context.
Timing belt maintenance figure that can support belt routing and timing belt context.
Idler pulley assembly maintenance figure that can support idler assembly identification and tensioning context.
Motor installation diagram showing motor mounting and clamp collar connection points, including motor electrical connector orientation down.
Counterbalance weight figure that can support counter-balance identification context.

steps.title: Insert the gearbox into the pilot hole
steps.purpose: Begin reinstallation of the Z-axis gearbox into its mounting location.
steps.instruction: Insert the gearbox into the pilot hole.
steps.expected_result: The gearbox enters the pilot hole and is positioned for further installation.
steps.healthy_condition: The gearbox fits into the pilot hole without obstruction.
steps.failure_condition: The gearbox cannot be inserted into the pilot hole.

actions.label: Insert gearbox
actions.exact_action: Insert the gearbox into the pilot hole.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gearbox visual reference for the component being inserted into the pilot hole.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the gearbox cannot be inserted into the pilot hole.

steps.title: Mount the timing pulley on the gearbox
steps.purpose: Install the timing pulley onto the gearbox using the referenced installation procedure.
steps.instruction: Mount a timing pulley on the gearbox using the referenced Installation procedure on page 168.
steps.expected_result: The timing pulley is mounted on the gearbox per the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The timing pulley is installed on the gearbox in accordance with the referenced installation procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The timing pulley cannot be mounted per the referenced installation procedure.

actions.label: Install timing pulley
actions.exact_action: Mount a timing pulley on the gearbox using the referenced Installation procedure on page 168.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the timing pulley cannot be mounted using the referenced installation procedure.

steps.title: Loop the belt over the pulley
steps.purpose: Reconnect the belt to the pulley before final seating and tensioning.
steps.instruction: Loop the belt over the pulley.
steps.expected_result: The belt is placed over the pulley.
steps.healthy_condition: The belt is routed over the pulley and ready for subsequent installation steps.
steps.failure_condition: The belt cannot be looped over the pulley or is not properly placed.

actions.label: Place belt over pulley
actions.exact_action: Loop the belt over the pulley.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt visual reference for belt routing over the pulley.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the belt cannot be looped over the pulley.

steps.title: Seat the gearbox and orient the clamp collar access hole up
steps.purpose: Fully position the gearbox and verify the required orientation before fastening.
steps.instruction: Fully seat the gearbox into the pilot hole and verify the clamp collar access hole is oriented up.
steps.expected_result: The gearbox is fully seated and the clamp collar access hole is oriented up.
steps.healthy_condition: The gearbox is fully seated in the pilot hole and the clamp collar access hole is oriented up as documented.
steps.failure_condition: The gearbox cannot be fully seated or the clamp collar access hole cannot be oriented up.

actions.label: Seat and orient gearbox
actions.exact_action: Fully seat the gearbox into the pilot hole and verify the clamp collar access hole is oriented up.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Clamp collar connection point context that may help identify the access hole orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the gearbox cannot be fully seated in the pilot hole.
Stop if the clamp collar access hole cannot be oriented up as documented.

steps.title: Fasten the gearbox with M6x20 screws
steps.purpose: Secure the gearbox to the assembly with the specified fasteners, thread treatment, and torque.
steps.instruction: Mount the gearbox with four M6x20 socket-head screws with Loctite® and tighten to 7 Nm (62 in-lb).
steps.expected_result: The gearbox is secured with four M6x20 socket-head screws tightened to the documented torque.
steps.healthy_condition: All four screws are installed with Loctite® and tightened to 7 Nm (62 in-lb).
steps.failure_condition: The gearbox cannot be secured with the specified screws or torque.

actions.label: Secure gearbox
actions.exact_action: Mount the gearbox with four M6x20 socket-head screws with Loctite® and tighten to 7 Nm (62 in-lb).

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Gearbox mounting context for the installed gearbox.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the gearbox cannot be secured with four M6x20 socket-head screws and Loctite®.
Stop if the specified 7 Nm (62 in-lb) torque cannot be applied.

steps.title: Mount the Z-axis motor with connectors oriented down
steps.purpose: Reinstall the motor onto the gearbox using the referenced motor installation procedure and verify required orientation.
steps.instruction: Mount the Z-axis motor using the referenced Motor Installation procedure on page 108 and verify the motor electrical connectors are oriented down.
steps.expected_result: The Z-axis motor is mounted and the electrical connectors are oriented down.
steps.healthy_condition: The motor is installed per the referenced procedure and the electrical connectors face down.
steps.failure_condition: The motor cannot be installed per the referenced procedure or the electrical connectors cannot be oriented down.

actions.label: Install motor
actions.exact_action: Mount the Z-axis motor using the referenced Motor Installation procedure on page 108 and verify the motor electrical connectors are oriented down.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Motor mounting and connector orientation; verify electrical connections face down.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the motor electrical connectors cannot be oriented down as documented.

steps.title: Lower the counter-balance carriage onto the lower hard stop
steps.purpose: Prepare the counter-balance carriage position for belt tensioning.
steps.instruction: Slightly lift the counter-balance carriage, remove the M6 fasteners supporting it, and lower the counter-balance carriage so it rests on the lower hard stop.
steps.expected_result: The counter-balance carriage rests on the lower hard stop.
steps.healthy_condition: The counter-balance carriage is lowered in a controlled manner and rests on the lower hard stop.
steps.failure_condition: The counter-balance carriage cannot be safely lowered to the lower hard stop.

actions.label: Lower counter-balance carriage
actions.exact_action: Slightly lift the counter-balance carriage, remove the M6 fasteners supporting it, and lower the counter-balance carriage so it rests on the lower hard stop.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counter-balance assembly context while positioning the carriage.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the counter-balance carriage cannot be safely supported or lowered.

steps.title: Loosen the idler pulley assembly for adjustment
steps.purpose: Set the idler pulley assembly to a lightly retained but adjustable state before tensioning.
steps.instruction: Very lightly tighten the M8x25 screws holding the idler pulley assembly so the assembly is not loose but can still slide under the fasteners.
steps.expected_result: The idler pulley assembly is retained but still able to slide for adjustment.
steps.healthy_condition: The assembly is not loose and can still slide under the fasteners.
steps.failure_condition: The assembly is either loose or cannot slide for adjustment.

actions.label: Set idler assembly for sliding adjustment
actions.exact_action: Very lightly tighten the M8x25 screws holding the idler pulley assembly so the assembly is not loose but can still slide under the fasteners.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly location and adjustment context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the idler pulley assembly cannot be set so it is not loose but can still slide.

steps.title: Thread the tension screws to firm contact
steps.purpose: Bring both tension screws into initial contact with the actuator base before applying tension.
steps.instruction: Thread the two M6x35 tension screws until they make firm contact with the actuator base.
steps.expected_result: Both tension screws make firm contact with the actuator base.
steps.healthy_condition: Each M6x35 tension screw is threaded to firm contact with the actuator base.
steps.failure_condition: One or both tension screws cannot be brought to firm contact with the actuator base.

actions.label: Bring tension screws to contact
actions.exact_action: Thread the two M6x35 tension screws until they make firm contact with the actuator base.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if either tension screw cannot make firm contact with the actuator base.

steps.title: Tighten both tension screws equally
steps.purpose: Apply even force to the belt tensioning mechanism.
steps.instruction: Continue to equally tighten the two tension screws, turning both screws the exact same amount so they apply the same amount of force.
steps.expected_result: Both tension screws are advanced equally and apply the same amount of force.
steps.healthy_condition: The two tension screws are turned the exact same amount.
steps.failure_condition: Equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained.

actions.label: Equalize tension screw adjustment
actions.exact_action: Continue to equally tighten the two tension screws, turning both screws the exact same amount so they apply the same amount of force.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained.

steps.title: Measure belt tension on the counter-balance leg
steps.purpose: Set belt tension to the documented target and acceptable range.
steps.instruction: Measure belt tension only on the counter-balance leg of the belt and gradually increase tension until 471 N is reached, with an acceptable range of 448 N to 494 N.
steps.expected_result: Belt tension reaches the documented target within the acceptable range.
steps.healthy_condition: Measured belt tension on the counter-balance leg is within 448 N to 494 N, targeting 471 N.
steps.failure_condition: Belt tension cannot be brought into the documented 448 N to 494 N range.

actions.label: Measure belt tension
actions.exact_action: Measure belt tension only on the counter-balance leg of the belt and gradually increase tension until 471 N is reached, with an acceptable range of 448 N to 494 N.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt context to identify the counter-balance leg of the belt.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if belt tension cannot be brought into the documented 448 N to 494 N range.

steps.title: Set sonic tension meter values if using a Gates 508c
steps.purpose: Configure the documented sonic tension meter parameters when that instrument is used.
steps.instruction: If using a Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter, use Mass = 4.86 g/m, Width = 20 mm, and Span = 1200 mm.
steps.expected_result: The Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter is configured with the documented values.
steps.healthy_condition: Mass is set to 4.86 g/m, Width to 20 mm, and Span to 1200 mm.
steps.failure_condition: The meter cannot be configured with the documented values when used.

actions.label: Configure sonic tension meter
actions.exact_action: If using a Gates 508c Sonic Tension Meter, use Mass = 4.86 g/m, Width = 20 mm, and Span = 1200 mm.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the sonic tension meter cannot be configured as documented when used.

steps.title: Verify three acceptable tension readings in a row
steps.purpose: Confirm stable and repeatable belt tension within the acceptable range.
steps.instruction: Adjust and check the tension until three acceptable readings in a row are received, noting that excessive area noise can interfere with sonic meter readings.
steps.expected_result: Three acceptable belt tension readings in a row are obtained.
steps.healthy_condition: Three consecutive acceptable readings are received without interference affecting the measurement.
steps.failure_condition: Three acceptable readings in a row cannot be obtained.

actions.label: Confirm repeatable tension readings
actions.exact_action: Adjust and check the tension until three acceptable readings in a row are received, noting that excessive area noise can interfere with sonic meter readings.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if three acceptable readings in a row cannot be obtained.
Escalate if excessive area noise prevents reliable sonic meter readings.

steps.title: Lock the idler assembly in place
steps.purpose: Secure the idler pulley assembly after belt tension has been set.
steps.instruction: Tighten the M8x25 fasteners to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs) to lock the idler assembly in place.
steps.expected_result: The idler assembly is locked in place at the documented torque.
steps.healthy_condition: The M8x25 fasteners are tightened to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs).
steps.failure_condition: The idler assembly cannot be locked in place at the documented torque.

actions.label: Torque idler fasteners
actions.exact_action: Tighten the M8x25 fasteners to 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs) to lock the idler assembly in place.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly being locked in place after tensioning.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the idler assembly cannot be locked in place at 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs).

steps.title: Raise the counter-balance carriage to mid-travel and reinstall support screws
steps.purpose: Return the counter-balance carriage to the documented position and restore the support screws.
steps.instruction: Raise the counter-balance carriage back to mid-travel and insert the M6 socket-head screws for the carriage to rest against.
steps.expected_result: The counter-balance carriage is at mid-travel and the M6 socket-head screws are inserted.
steps.healthy_condition: The carriage is raised to mid-travel and rests against the inserted M6 socket-head screws.
steps.failure_condition: The carriage cannot be raised to mid-travel or the support screws cannot be inserted.

actions.label: Restore carriage support screws
actions.exact_action: Raise the counter-balance carriage back to mid-travel and insert the M6 socket-head screws for the carriage to rest against.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Counter-balance assembly context while raising the carriage to mid-travel.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the counter-balance carriage cannot be raised to mid-travel or supported as described.

steps.title: Reattach the A-axis belt clamp
steps.purpose: Secure the A-axis belt clamp without damaging or misaligning the belt.
steps.instruction: Re-attach the A-axis belt clamp using six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the belt clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt. Move the belt slightly if needed for alignment.
steps.expected_result: The A-axis belt clamp is reattached and aligned with the belt teeth without pinching the belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The belt clamp aligns with the belt teeth and the belt is not pinched.
steps.failure_condition: The belt clamp cannot align with the belt teeth or the belt is pinched.

actions.label: Install A-axis belt clamp
actions.exact_action: Re-attach the A-axis belt clamp using six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the belt clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt. Move the belt slightly if needed for alignment.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Belt tooth alignment context while reattaching the A-axis belt clamp.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the belt clamp cannot align with the belt teeth.
Stop if the belt is being pinched.

steps.title: Reattach the counterbalance belt clamp
steps.purpose: Secure the counterbalance belt clamp without damaging or misaligning the belt.
steps.instruction: Re-attach the counterbalance belt clamp using six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the belt clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt. Lift the counterbalance slightly if needed for alignment.
steps.expected_result: The counterbalance belt clamp is reattached and aligned with the belt teeth without pinching the belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The belt clamp aligns with the belt teeth and the belt is not pinched.
steps.failure_condition: The belt clamp cannot align with the belt teeth or the belt is pinched.

actions.label: Install counterbalance belt clamp
actions.exact_action: Re-attach the counterbalance belt clamp using six M6 screws and Loctite®, making sure the belt clamp aligns with the belt teeth and does not pinch the belt. Lift the counterbalance slightly if needed for alignment.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Belt tooth alignment context while reattaching the counterbalance belt clamp.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the belt clamp cannot align with the belt teeth.
Stop if the belt is being pinched.

steps.title: Remove the support screws from the A-axis and counter-balance
steps.purpose: Return the A-axis and counter-balance from temporary support to normal installed condition.
steps.instruction: Remove the M6 socket-head screws supporting the A-axis and counter-balance.
steps.expected_result: The temporary support screws are removed from the A-axis and counter-balance.
steps.healthy_condition: No temporary M6 support screws remain installed.
steps.failure_condition: The support screws cannot be removed or the A-axis and counter-balance cannot remain in the installed state after removal.

actions.label: Remove temporary supports
actions.exact_action: Remove the M6 socket-head screws supporting the A-axis and counter-balance.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the support screws cannot be removed safely.

steps.title: Reinstall the top and bottom side panels
steps.purpose: Restore the machine enclosure after mechanical installation is complete.
steps.instruction: Reinstall the top and bottom side panels.
steps.expected_result: The top and bottom side panels are reinstalled.
steps.healthy_condition: Both side panels are installed and the assembly is closed up.
steps.failure_condition: The top and bottom side panels cannot be reinstalled.

actions.label: Reinstall side panels
actions.exact_action: Reinstall the top and bottom side panels.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the top and bottom side panels cannot be reinstalled.

steps.title: Restart the operator station
steps.purpose: Return the operator station to service using the referenced startup procedure.
steps.instruction: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure on page 66.
steps.expected_result: The operator station is restarted using the referenced procedure.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator station restart is completed per the referenced procedure.
steps.failure_condition: The operator station cannot be restarted using the referenced procedure.

actions.label: Restart operator station
actions.exact_action: Re-start the operator station using the referenced Starting The Operator Station procedure on page 66.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the operator station cannot be restarted using the referenced procedure.

success_criteria: The gearbox is fully seated in the pilot hole.
The clamp collar access hole is oriented up as documented.
The timing pulley and belt are installed.
The Z-axis motor is installed with electrical connectors oriented down.
Belt tension is set to 471 N target with an acceptable range of 448 N to 494 N.
Three acceptable belt tension readings in a row are obtained.
The idler assembly is locked in place.
The A-axis belt clamp and counterbalance belt clamp are reattached without pinching the belt and aligned with the belt teeth.
The top and bottom side panels are reinstalled.
healthy_conditions: Gearbox fully seated in the pilot hole.
Clamp collar access hole oriented up.
Idler pulley assembly retained for adjustment, then locked at 21.2 Nm (188 in-lbs).
Belt tension measured on the counter-balance leg is within 448 N to 494 N.
Three acceptable tension readings in a row are obtained.
Belt clamps align with belt teeth and do not pinch the belt.
failure_conditions: Gearbox cannot be fully seated in the pilot hole.
Clamp collar access hole cannot be oriented up as documented.
Motor electrical connectors cannot be oriented down as documented.
Equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained.
Belt tension cannot be brought into the documented 448 N to 494 N range.
Three acceptable readings in a row cannot be obtained.
Excessive area noise interferes with sonic meter readings.
The belt clamp cannot align with the belt teeth.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if the gearbox cannot be fully seated in the pilot hole.
Stop and escalate if the clamp collar access hole cannot be oriented up as documented.
Stop and escalate if the motor electrical connectors cannot be oriented down as documented.
Stop and escalate if equal adjustment of the two tension screws cannot be maintained.
Stop and escalate if belt tension cannot be brought into the documented 448 N to 494 N range.
Stop and escalate if three acceptable readings in a row cannot be obtained.
Account for excessive area noise if sonic meter readings are unreliable.
Stop and escalate if either belt clamp cannot align with the belt teeth or if the belt is pinched.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Timing belt routing and belt clamp tooth alignment context.
Idler pulley assembly location and locking context.
Motor mounting, clamp collar connection points, and motor electrical connector orientation down.
Counter-balance assembly context during carriage positioning.

missing_details: The packet does not provide the full source section text for pages 134-139, so step-level quotations could not be added beyond candidate wording and artifact summaries.
The packet does not explicitly state whether production stop is required.
The packet does not explicitly state whether LOTO is required for this installation section.
No source-supported estimated time is provided for this procedure.
No directly matching artifact for the exact pilot hole insertion view or belt clamp alignment figures was provided; related visuals were attached where relevant.
